LessonCache.title: LessonCache
LessonCache.description: Stores dynamically generated language lessons to ensure fast retrieval and avoid regenerating content for every user. These lessons are shared across all users.

id.description: Unique identifier for the LessonCache entity, typically formatted as '{language}_{path}_week{N}' (e.g., 'french_survival_week1').

language.description: The target language for this lesson (e.g., 'French').

path.description: The learning path this lesson belongs to (e.g., 'survival', 'alphabet', 'numbers').

week.description: The week number of the lesson within its learning path (e.g., 1).

lessonJson.description: The complete lesson content as a stringified JSON object, containing all days, vocabulary, dialogues, and exercises for the week. This field stores the full lesson structure generated by the AI.

cachedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when this lesson was generated and stored in the cache.

UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Stores individual user preferences, settings, and overall account information.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity, matching the Firebase Authentication user ID (uid).

email.description: The user's email address, used for identification. (Security Best Practice: Passwords and sensitive authentication data are handled by an external authentication system like Firebase Auth).

nativeLanguage.description: The user's selected native language, used for lesson translations and customization (e.g., 'English').

selectedLanguage.description: The target language the user is currently learning (e.g., 'French').

subscriptionType.description: The user's current subscription level: 'free', 'monthly', or 'yearly'.

subscriptionExpiryDate.description: The date when the user's premium subscription is set to expire, if applicable.

xpPoints.description: Total experience points accumulated by the user, contributing to their learning level.

currentStreak.description: The number of consecutive days the user has completed at least one lesson.

lastActiveDate.description: The date of the user's last recorded activity, used for streak calculation.

aiPlanningEnabled.description: A flag indicating whether the user has enabled AI to automatically plan their weekly lesson themes.

UserLessonProgress.title: UserLessonProgress
UserLessonProgress.description: Tracks a user's completion and performance for a specific daily lesson.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserLessonProgress entity, typically formatted as '{userId}_{language}_{path}_week{N}_day{N}' (e.g., 'user123_french_survival_week1_day1').

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile entity. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N UserLessonProgress)

language.description: The target language of the completed lesson (e.g., 'French').

path.description: The learning path of the completed lesson (e.g., 'survival').

week.description: The week number of the completed lesson.

day.description: The day number within the week for the completed lesson.

completed.description: Indicates if the specific daily lesson has been fully completed by the user.

score.description: The user's score (0-100) on the exercises for this daily lesson.

completedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when this daily lesson was completed.

exerciseResults.description: A stringified JSON object containing detailed results of the exercises for this day, such as correct/incorrect answers for each question. This provides granular feedback without nesting full objects.

UserWeekProgress.title: UserWeekProgress
UserWeekProgress.description: Tracks a user's overall progress and selected topic for a specific learning week within a given language and path.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserWeekProgress entity, typically formatted as '{userId}_{language}_{path}_week{N}' (e.g., 'user123_french_survival_week1').

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile entity. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N UserWeekProgress)

language.description: The target language for this week's progress (e.g., 'French').

path.description: The learning path for this week's progress (e.g., 'survival').

week.description: The week number being tracked for progress.

daysCompleted.description: An array of numbers representing the days (1-7) within this week that have been marked as completed by the user.

weekCompleted.description: Indicates if all 7 days of this specific week have been completed by the user.

selectedTopic.description: The theme chosen (by user or AI) for this particular week's lessons, if AI planning is enabled or a topic was explicitly selected.

definition.description: Stores individual user preferences, settings, and overall account information. Access is strictly restricted to the authenticated user whose UID matches the 'userId' path parameter. The document's 'id' field is denormalized to explicitly match the 'userId' for clear ownership and debuggability.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication User ID (UID) of the user, which also serves as the document ID.

definition.description: Stores dynamically generated language lessons, shared across all users for rapid retrieval and consistency. All documents in this collection are publicly readable. Write (create, update, delete) access is restricted to authenticated administrators only. The document ID, 'lessonCacheId', is a composite string formed from '{language}_{path}_week{N}' and matches the 'id' field within the document.

params.name: lessonCacheId
params.description: The unique identifier for the cached lesson, typically a composite string like 'french_survival_week1'.

definition.description: Tracks a user's completion and performance for a specific daily lesson. Access is strictly restricted to the authenticated user whose UID matches the 'userId' path parameter. The document includes a denormalized 'userId' field for explicit ownership and data integrity. The document ID, 'lessonProgressId', is a composite string matching the 'id' field within the document (e.g., 'user123_french_survival_week1_day1').

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication User ID (UID) of the user.

params.name: lessonProgressId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user's daily lesson progress, typically a composite string like '{userId}_{language}_{path}_week{N}_day{N}', also serving as the document ID.

definition.description: Tracks a user's overall progress and selected topic for a specific learning week within a given language and path. Access is strictly restricted to the authenticated user whose UID matches the 'userId' path parameter. The document includes a denormalized 'userId' field for explicit ownership and data integrity. The document ID, 'userWeekProgressId', is a composite string matching the 'id' field within the document (e.g., 'user123_french_survival_week1').

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication User ID (UID) of the user.

params.name: userWeekProgressId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user's weekly progress, typically a composite string like '{userId}_{language}_{path}_week{N}', also serving as the document ID.

definition.description: A collection storing Firebase Authentication UIDs of users designated as administrators. The mere existence of a document in this collection, where 'adminId' matches a user's UID, grants administrative privileges. This follows the 'existence over content' principle for role-based access control. Documents can be empty or contain only an 'id' field matching 'adminId'.

params.name: adminId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication User ID (UID) of an administrator user, which also serves as the document ID.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure for LingoForge is meticulously designed to maximize security, scalability, and debuggability, adhering strictly to the core design principles and strategy mandates. This approach significantly minimizes complexity in security rules and ensures robust data integrity.

**1. Authorization Independence via Denormalization:**
To eliminate the need for `get()` calls in security rules—a critical requirement for robust, atomic operations—authorization context is consistently denormalized. For `UserLessonProgress` and `UserWeekProgress` documents, the `userId` field is explicitly included within each document. While the primary authorization is established by the path (`/userProgress/{userId}/lessons/{lessonId}` and `/userProgress/{userId}/weeks/{weekId}`), the denormalized `userId` within the document serves as an explicit, immutable declaration of ownership. This allows for straightforward security rules such as `allow read, write: if request.auth.uid == userId && resource.data.userId == userId;`, making authorization clear, easy to debug, and inherently independent of parent document fetches.

**2. Clarity of Intent (Debuggability):**
The structure prioritizes explicit and descriptive naming for all collections, subcollections, and wildcards. For example, `/userProfiles/{userId}` clearly indicates individual user data, `/lessonCache/{lessonCacheId}` denotes cached, shared lessons, and `/userProgress/{userId}/lessons/{lessonId}` precisely tracks user-specific daily lesson progress. This semantic clarity greatly simplifies security rule writing, data retrieval, and overall system debugging.

**3. DBAC (Database Access Control) and Global Roles:**
Instead of relying on custom claims, all authorization roles are stored directly in Firestore. A dedicated top-level collection, `/adminUsers/{adminId}`, is introduced to manage administrator privileges. The *existence* of a document at `adminUsers/{request.auth.uid}` is the sole determinant of admin status, fully implementing the 'existence over content' principle. This enables simple, secure, and highly scalable security rules for administrative actions, such as `allow create, update: if exists(/databases/$(database)/documents/adminUsers/$(request.auth.uid));` for modifying the global `lessonCache`.

**4. QAPs (Queries Are Not Filters):**
The structural design inherently supports secure `list` operations, preventing unauthorized data leakage without complex rule-based filtering. All user-specific data (`UserProfile`, `UserLessonProgress`, `UserWeekProgress`) is located within paths prefixed by the user's UID (`/userProfiles/{userId}/...` and `/userProgress/{userId}/...`). This ensures that any `list` query initiated by an authenticated user (`request.auth.uid`) can only retrieve documents strictly within their own designated data paths. The security rules will simply verify `request.auth.uid == userId`, guaranteeing that no user can inadvertently or maliciously access another user's private data. For shared data in `/lessonCache/{lessonCacheId}`, a simple `allow read;` rule suffices, as all documents are intended for public access, meeting QAPs for shared content.

**5. Invariants and Data Integrity:**
Ownership is rigorously enforced through path-based authorization. Timestamps (`cachedAt`, `completedAt`, `lastActiveDate`) are explicitly included in relevant entities, allowing security rules to enforce server-side validation. The denormalization of `userId` in progress documents, matching the path parameter, reinforces data consistency and simplifies integrity checks.

**6. Structural Segregation (Homogeneous Security Posture):**
Each top-level collection and its subcollections are designed with a uniform security posture:
*   `/lessonCache`: Public read, admin-only write.
*   `/userProfiles/{userId}`: Owner-only read/write.
*   `/userProgress/{userId}/lessons/{lessonId}` and `/userProgress/{userId}/weeks/{weekId}`: Owner-only read/write.
*   `/adminUsers/{adminId}`: Used solely for admin existence checks. 
This segregation dramatically simplifies security rules, as each collection has a clear and consistent access model.

**7. Access Modeling & Predictability:**
LingoForge standardizes on path-based ownership for private user data and a global read model for public, cached lessons. State modeling is explicit, using fields like `completed`, `weekCompleted`, `subscriptionType`, and `aiPlanningEnabled`. Schemas are predictable, avoiding dynamic keys, and document IDs for `LessonCache`, `UserLessonProgress`, and `UserWeekProgress` are semantically meaningful composite strings, enhancing clarity and query efficiency. This comprehensive design ensures a secure, scalable, and maintainable data foundation for LingoForge.
